search_text: 1909.13536 Dilworth Garrigos Hernandez Kutzarova Temlyakov Lebesgue-type inequalities in greedy approximation Remark 2.11 asks whether property D(s,c_1) can hold for some non-uniformly smooth Banach space and some dictionary. Partial result: in every nontrivial l1 direct sum E plus_1 F, every unit atom phi fails the D(s,c) distance inequality for every s>1 and c>0. Therefore no nonempty dictionary in such an l1 sum can satisfy property D(s,c). This rules out nonsmooth l1-sum corner constructions but does not settle the full existence question.
